quotes.text: Price is what you pay, value is what you get.
It's far better to buy a wonderful company at a fair price than a fair company at a wonderful price.
The key to investing is determining the competitive advantage of any given company and, above all, the durability of that advantage.
What counts for most people in investing is not how much they know, but rather how realistically they define what they don't know.
The three most important words in investing are margin of safety.

Owner earnings represent (a) reported earnings plus (b) depreciation, depletion, amortization, and certain other non-cash charges less (c) the average annual amount of capitalized expenditures for plant and equipment.
The most important thing to do if you find yourself in a hole is to stop digging.
Our favorite holding period is forever.
We look for businesses that are run by able and honest managers and that have earned good returns on equity while employing either no debt or very little debt.
When we own portions of outstanding businesses with outstanding managements, our favorite holding period is forever.
Invert, always invert. Turn a situation or problem upside down. Look at it backward.
Simplicity has a way of improving performance through enabling us to better understand what we are doing.
How do you compete against a true fanatic? You can only try to build the best possible moat and continuously attempt to widen it.
Knowing what you don't know is more useful than being brilliant.
